name: page-header
role: Top-level in-app page-header strip rendered below pattern-nav-app-chrome. Hosts eyebrow + title + subtitle + optional breadcrumb + meta cluster + action cluster. Three registers — minimal (just title), default (title + actions), extended (everything: eyebrow + breadcrumb + title + subtitle + meta + actions + optional tab-strip). Three sizes — sm (compact), md (default), lg (hero-ish; used on flagship page entries).

semanticStructure: (optional) <nav data-pattern='app-page-tabs'> — composing from nav-app-chrome's tabs primitive
meta.headingHierarchy: page-header-title uses <h1> when the page-header is the FIRST heading on the page (typical for app-shell pages where nav-app-chrome contains no headings). Use <h2> when the page already has an <h1> elsewhere (e.g., when embedded inside a marketing template that has its own hero h1).
meta.navAppChromeRelationship: Designed to sit BELOW pattern-nav-app-chrome. The chrome handles workspace-level identity (workspace switcher, top breadcrumb showing Workspace > Project); this page-header handles page-level identity (the page title + page-level actions). They don't overlap responsibilities.
meta.tabsCoupling: When the register is 'extended' and the page has sub-sections, page-header may host an <app-page-tabs> instance composed from pattern-nav-app-chrome. This is a sibling-pack composition — nav-app-chrome's app-page-tabs is general enough to live below the page-header as well as below the chrome.

ariaPatterns.noBannerLandmark: page-header is just a <header> element WITHOUT role='banner'. The role='banner' lives on the parent nav-app-chrome (only ONE banner landmark per page).
ariaPatterns.ariaLabelledby: If the page-header has descendant interactive regions (action cluster, tabs), they get their own role + aria-label per the contracts of the composed patterns.

name: page-header-eyebrow
role: Small uppercase pre-heading rendered above the title. Used for category breadcrumbs ('Settings · Billing'), section names, or status badges. Renders as a small text strip; may include a breadcrumb-style chevron-separated path.

registers.text: Single line of uppercase text. Used as a categoricalbreadcrumb-lite ('Settings · Billing' / 'Notes · Drafts').
registers.breadcrumb: Composes app-breadcrumb from nav-app-chrome — renders chevron-separated segments with the last as the current page (aria-current='page').
registers.badge-row: Inline row of status badges (using the same status-badge styling as data-table-cell's status-badge content type).

ariaPatterns.breadcrumbAria: When register='breadcrumb', the inner <nav aria-label='Breadcrumb'> + <ol> structure follows the same contract as nav-app-chrome's app-breadcrumb.

name: page-header-title-block
role: Wrapper that groups the title + subtitle. Allows the title and subtitle to share a single flex column so the meta cluster and action cluster can align relative to the visual centre of the block.

name: page-header-title
role: The page title. Sized per parent's data-size attribute. h1 by default (when no other h1 on page); h2 when the page is nested within a larger context (e.g., marketing template with its own h1).

meta.textWrapBalance: Apply text-wrap: balance for visually-balanced multi-line titles.
meta.editableInPlace: If the page-header is editable (e.g., document name in Galley Notes), the title element can be contenteditable + role='textbox' aria-label='Document title'. Inline editing behaviour is out of scope for v1 — host page provides.

name: page-header-subtitle
role: Descriptive subtitle below the title. 1-2 lines max. Used for page descriptions ('Track every recurring charge across all Galley tools'), document tag lines, or admin-style descriptions.

meta.textWrapPretty: Apply text-wrap: pretty for orphan-avoidance on multi-line subtitles.

name: page-header-meta
role: Metadata cluster below or beside the title. Hosts owner avatar (composing avatar-stack from vocab-app-shell), last-updated timestamp, badges, status pills, member count. Renders as an inline-flex row.

semanticStructure: <avatar-stack> — composes from vocab-app-shell. 'Owned by Elena Park' or 'Shared with 3 people'

ariaPatterns.avatarStackLabel: avatar-stack inherits its aria-label from vocab-app-shell ('Owned by Elena Park' / 'Shared with 3 people').

name: page-header-actions
role: Action button cluster on the right side of the page-header. Composes <action-button> from pattern-button-system. Typical pattern: 1-2 ghost actions (Share, Filter, Export) + 1 primary action (Add, Save, Publish). Optional kebab menu for overflow.

registers.primary-only: Single primary CTA ('Add subscription', 'New document'). Used on simple list views.
registers.secondary-plus-primary: Ghost / secondary action + primary action ('Share' + 'Save', 'Export' + 'New'). The 80% choice.
registers.extended: Multiple ghost actions + secondary + primary + overflow kebab menu. Used on power-user surfaces (admin dashboards, multi-action editors).

meta.responsiveBehaviour: At narrow viewport widths, the action cluster collapses to a single overflow kebab menu containing every action. The primary CTA may stay visible while secondaries collapse, depending on viewport width.

ariaPatterns.groupRole: <div role='group' aria-label='Page actions'> wraps the cluster.
ariaPatterns.iconOnlyAtNarrow: When collapsed at narrow widths, icon-only buttons must have explicit aria-label.
